message.content:
Search GitHub for open source Claude Code extensions/plugins/MCP servers that can check or display Claude subscription remaining quota, usage limits, or rate limits in real-time. Look for repositories related to:
- Claude Code usage tracking
- Claude subscription quota checker
- Claude rate limit monitor
- Claude Code statusline plugins for usage display

Search on GitHub and the web for any community-built tools or MCP servers that provide this functionality.

Based on the web search results from GitHub, I found several resources related to Claude Code plugin extensions and usage limits:

## Plugin Extensions Overview

Claude Code plugins are extensions that enhance Claude Code with custom slash commands, specialized agents, hooks, and MCP servers. These official Claude Code plugins extend functionality through custom commands, agents, and workflows, with many more plugins available through community marketplaces.

## Usage Limit Tracking

Several community-developed tools and extensions on GitHub focus on tracking Claude Code usage limits:

- The claude-code-limit-tracker is a comprehensive usage tracking system for Claude Code that displays real-time quota usage per model with accurate subscription limits that display in your status line.

- Claude Usage Extensions help monitor quota usage and calculate token consumption from various sources including uploaded files, project knowledge, chat history, and AI responses.

- Browser extensions like claude-usage-monitor monitor Claude usage limits with visual charts and periodic updates.

## Additional Resources

The anthropics/claude-plugins-official repository is a curated directory of high-quality plugins for Claude Code. You can explore the official plugins repository and community-built extensions to find tools that help you track and manage your Claude Code usage limits effectively.

Based on the search results, I found several tools and solutions for tracking Claude Code subscription quota usage in your statusline:

## Main Tools & Plugins

1. **claude-code-limit-tracker** - A comprehensive tracking system that displays real-time quota usage per model with accurate subscription limits that display in your status line. It shows actual conversation hours and can be installed via git clone.

2. **claude-quota-tracker** - Tracks Claude.ai subscription usage directly in VS Code with real-time quota monitoring, perfect as a companion for Claude Code users.

3. **ccusage** - Provides a compact, real-time view of Claude Code usage designed to integrate with Claude Code's status line hooks. It shows current session cost, today's total cost, current session block, burn rate, and active model.

4. **Claude Code Usage Plugin** - An IntelliJ IDEs plugin that displays subscription usage limits in the IDE status bar, tracking subscription quota usage only (5-hour, 7-day limits).

5. **Claude-Usage-Tracker** - A macOS menu bar app with statusline integration that displays usage percentage, model name, context window, profile name, git branch, and working directory.

## Official Claude Code Support

Claude Code provides a /statusline command that accepts natural language instructions describing what you want displayed and generates a script file in ~/.claude/ and updates your settings automatically.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude MCP server rate limit monitoring and usage quota:

## Rate Limit Monitoring

You can monitor your rate limit usage on the Usage page of the Claude Console, which provides token and request charts along with two separate rate limit charts. Additionally, every API response from Anthropic includes rate limit headers: anthropic-ratelimit-requests-remaining shows how many requests you have left in the current minute window, anthropic-ratelimit-tokens-remaining shows your remaining token budget, and anthropic-ratelimit-tokens-reset provides a timestamp for when limits replenish.

## Usage Quota Tracking

Unlike some SaaS systems, Claude does not provide detailed per-prompt or per-token breakdowns, requiring developers to self-monitor usage, and teams often maintain manual tracking or use custom scripts to estimate remaining capacity.

For specialized monitoring, there's a quota tracking dashboard that's a mitmproxy-based monitoring UI which passively intercepts Claude API responses and extracts rate limit data from Anthropic's own HTTP headers, providing real-time status cards with current 5-hour and 7-day quota utilization, live burn rate calculation, and estimated time to 100% exhaustion.

## Rate Limit Structure

Claude Code rate limits operate as three independent systems — RPM, TPM, and daily/weekly quotas. Session limits are based on a rolling 5-hour window of token usage, and unlike a daily cap that resets at midnight, Claude Code uses a sliding window where the tokens used exactly 5 hours ago start becoming available again, continuously.

Based on the search results, here are several open-source options for tracking Claude API usage with dashboards:

## Key Open-Source Solutions

**Prism — Claude API Cost & Usage Analytics**
Prism is a drop-in proxy that tracks every Claude API call — cost, tokens, latency, by team. It works as a FastAPI proxy with PostgreSQL logging and a React dashboard, functioning as a drop-in replacement where you change your base_url from api.anthropic.com to your Prism instance, and everything else stays the same — same SDK, same headers, same streaming.

**Claude Usage Tracker**
Claude Usage Tracker auto-detects 9+ tools, scans local session data, and shows everything in one dashboard: daily costs, model breakdowns, heatmaps, session logs, and monthly projections, available as a native macOS app or browser mode for other OS devices with no cloud, no accounts, and no telemetry.

**Claude Code OpenTelemetry Integration**
Claude Code CLI emits telemetry via OpenTelemetry when enabled through docker compose, which collects, stores, and visualizes metrics with Prometheus for real-time metrics (tokens, cost, latency).

**claude-dashboard Plugin**
A comprehensive status line plugin for Claude Code with context usage, API rate limits, and cost tracking with a modular widget system.

All of these tools focus on providing detailed usage tracking, cost analysis, and visual dashboards for managing Claude API consumption across different contexts.

content.text:
Based on my search, I found several open-source Claude Code extensions, plugins, and MCP servers that track subscription quota, usage limits, and rate limits in real-time:

## Statusline Plugins & Tools

**1. claude-code-limit-tracker** - Comprehensive real-time quota tracking
- Displays real-time quota usage per model with accurate subscription limits
- Integrates directly into your Claude Code statusline
- Shows conversation hours and usage data
- Install via git clone and configure in settings.json

**2. ccusage** - Real-time usage monitoring for statusline
- Compact, real-time view of Claude Code usage
- Shows current session cost, daily total cost, current session block, burn rate
- Designed to integrate with Claude Code's statusline hooks
- Available with practical examples at ccusage.com

**3. claude-dashboard** - Comprehensive status line plugin
- Displays context usage, API rate limits, and cost tracking
- Modular widget system for statusline display
- Includes rate limit monitoring

## Native Applications

**4. Claude-Usage-Tracker** - macOS menu bar app
- Native Swift/SwiftUI application for real-time usage tracking
- Displays usage percentage, model name, context window, profile name
- Statusline integration support

## Browser Extensions

**5. claude-usage-extension** - VS Code/browser extension
- Shows Claude Pro/Max usage limits across Windows/Linux/macOS
- Real-time quota monitoring

**6. claude-usage-monitor** - Browser extension
- Monitors Claude usage limits with visual charts
- Periodic updates and visual tracking

## Open-Source Analytics Dashboards

**7. claude-usage-analytics** - Open-source cost & usage proxy
- FinOps for AI with comprehensive analytics
- Tracks spend, tokens, and latency across teams and models

**8. Claude API Usage Cost API** - Official Anthropic solution
- Built-in usage tracking in the Claude API
- Provides detailed cost and usage metrics

## How to Implement

With your current Claude Code setup, you can:

1. Install `claude-code-limit-tracker` or `ccusage` via the plugins marketplace
2. Use `/statusline` command to configure what usage data displays
3. Configure in your `settings.json` with custom statusline hooks

Your setup already has `claude-hud` configured in your statusline, which could be extended or combined with these quota-tracking plugins.

Sources:
- [claude-code-limit-tracker](https://github.com/TylerGallenbeck/claude-code-limit-tracker)
- [claude-quota-tracker](https://github.com/jonis100/claude-quota-tracker)
- [ccusage](https://ccusage.com/guide/statusline)
- [Claude Code Usage Plugin for JetBrains](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/29946-claude-code-usage)
- [Claude-Usage-Tracker macOS app](https://github.com/hamed-elfayome/Claude-Usage-Tracker)
- [claude-usage-extension](https://github.com/cfranci/claude-usage-extension)
- [claude-usage-monitor](https://github.com/oov/claude-usage-monitor)
- [claude-usage-analytics](https://github.com/reshevyas-png/claude-usage-analytics)
- [claude-dashboard](https://github.com/uppinote20/claude-dashboard)
- [Claude Code Statusline Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline)
- [How to Show Claude Code Usage Limits in Your Statusline](https://codelynx.dev/posts/claude-code-usage-limits-statusline)
